# Work Packet ALB-007-R1 — Guard: Fix Normalizer Self-Defeat + Literal-Form Hardening

## 1. TASK

Fix a correctness defect in `plugin/scripts/pre-tool-guard.sh` (the normalizer allows `\rm -rf /`) and add a bounded set of low-false-positive, literal-form deny rules identified by the adversarial red-team. Do NOT add heuristic detection.

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME

- `plugin/scripts/pre-tool-guard.sh` updated; `tests/test_guard.sh` extended.

## 3. CONTEXT

- **Read first:** `CONVENTIONS.md`, the current `plugin/scripts/pre-tool-guard.sh` in full, `docs/build/log/007-adversarial-hooks.md`, `docs/security-model.md` (what this guard is and is NOT — a best-effort speed-bump, not a security boundary).
- **The correctness defect (must fix):** an unconditional `printf '%b'` (decode_escapes, ~line 85) runs *before* the intended backslash-strip (~line 90, `sed 's/\\//g'`). For input `\rm -rf /`, `printf '%b'` interprets `\r` as a carriage return, which `tr` then collapses to a space → `rm` becomes ` m` → `match_rm_root` never fires → the command is ALLOWED. Verified: `\rm -rf /` deletes the filesystem root exactly like `rm -rf /`.
- **Invocation for testing:** `echo '{"session_id":"s","tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"<cmd>"}}' | bash plugin/scripts/pre-tool-guard.sh` — DENY = stdout JSON with `"permissionDecision":"deny"`; ALLOW = empty stdout.

## 4. MUST DO

**Correctness fix:** reorder or remove the unconditional `printf '%b'` so that a leading backslash-escaped command (`\rm`, and the same class `\t`/`\n`-prefixed) reduces to its real command and is matched. The targeted `$'...'`/`\xNN`/`\NNN` decoding that already exists must be preserved. After the fix, `\rm -rf /` must DENY.

**Literal-form hardening (each is an extension of an existing rule, no heuristics):**
1. Add `env|timeout|nohup|nice|setsid|stdbuf` to the command-wrapper alternation so `env rm -rf /`, `timeout 60 rm -rf /`, etc. are recognized (the wrapper consumes its own args before the real command). Keep existing wrappers.
2. `match_eval_command_substitution`: also match the backtick form `` eval `...` `` (currently only `eval $(...)`).
3. `match_git_force_protected`: also match force-push via refspec, `git push <remote> +<protected>` (e.g. `+main`, `+master`), not only `--force`/`-f`.
4. `contains_fork_bomb`: generalize from the single literal `:(){ :|:&};:` to the structural shape `NAME(){ NAME|NAME& };NAME` for any function name (normalize whitespace first). The classic `:` form must still DENY.
5. `match_pipe_to_shell`: broaden the post-pipe stage to also catch `| sudo sh`, `| tee … | sh` (an intermediate stage before the shell), and `|& sh`.
6. Add `/etc`, `/home`, `/usr`, `/var` (and their `/*` forms) to the recursive-removal `root_target` set, so `rm -rf /etc` DENYes. Keep `/`, `~`, `$HOME`.
7. Add a rule for `find <path> -delete` and `find <path> -exec rm` targeting `/` or the top-level system dirs above.

**Tests:** for every item 1–7 add a dangerous-command case asserting DENY with a sensible reason substring; keep the correctness case (`\rm -rf /` → DENY). Re-assert that all 8 captured `PreToolUse` payloads and a set of legitimate commands (`rm -rf ./build`, `git push origin main`, `env NODE_ENV=prod npm start`, `find . -name '*.tmp' -delete`) still ALLOW — **no false positives.**

## 5. MUST NOT DO

- Do NOT attempt to catch runtime-only obfuscations (`${IFS}`, `a=rm; $a`, `xargs`-supplied targets) — these are documented limitations in `docs/security-model.md`; a denylist cannot enumerate them without false positives, and ALB-007 forbids heuristic denials.
- Do NOT change the fail-open behavior (malformed stdin still ALLOWs and logs).
- Do NOT deny any of the legitimate commands listed above (false positives are worse than the gaps).
- Do NOT modify files outside `plugin/scripts/pre-tool-guard.sh` and `tests/test_guard.sh`.
- Do NOT run git state-changing commands.

## 6. TOOLS ALLOWED

File creation/editing and shell execution within the repository workspace only.

## 7. SUCCESS CRITERIA

- [ ] `bash tests/run.sh` exits 0; `\rm -rf /` and all 7 hardening classes DENY; captured payloads and the legitimate set ALLOW.
- [ ] Fail-open preserved; CI-equivalent shellcheck clean.
- [ ] `git status` shows only the two allowed paths.
